description_long: This is a wooden picket fence section composed of vertical planks with pointed tops, arranged side by side to form a continuous barrier. The wood grain texture is visible, giving the fence a rustic and natural appearance, with a dark brown stain that enhances the wood's character. Each plank is evenly spaced and appears to be fastened together by horizontal rails on the unseen side. The top edge of the fence features a uniform pointed pattern, with each plank cut to a sharp peak. There are no visible decorative elements or post caps, and the construction is simple and utilitarian. This type of fence is commonly used in residential yards, parks, or along sidewalks to demarcate boundaries and provide a modest level of privacy or security.
description: A dark brown wooden picket fence with pointed tops and visible wood grain, arranged in a simple, rustic style. The fence is solid and functional, with no decorative elements or post caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full row of pointed wooden pickets and the wood grain texture.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the alignment of the vertical planks.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the pointed tops and wood texture of the planks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow thickness and the arrangement of the pickets.
